title: Model with Arrays
essential_question: How can you use arrays to solve division problems?
strategies: Place tiles in rows of the given number, count rows.
Or place tiles in the given number of rows, count columns.
Write division and multiplication equations for the array.

pretest.stem: An array has 3 rows of 8 tiles. How many tiles in all?
24 tiles arranged in 4 rows. How many in each row?
An array has 5 columns with 7 tiles each. Total tiles?
36 tiles in 6 rows. How many columns?
An array shows 8 rows with 9 tiles each. Write the division: 72 ÷ 8 = ?

learn.title: Arrays and Division
learn.content: An array is objects arranged in equal rows and columns. You can use arrays to show division. 24 tiles in 4 rows means 24 ÷ 4 = 6 tiles per row.

learn.title: Building an Array
learn.content: To solve 18 ÷ 3: make 3 rows with 18 tiles. Keep adding tiles evenly to each row. Each row gets 6 tiles.

learn.title: Multiplication and Division
learn.content: The same array shows both: 4 × 6 = 24 and 24 ÷ 4 = 6. They are related facts.

try.stem: 20 tiles in 5 rows. How many per row?
An array has 6 rows and 7 columns. Total tiles?

practice_r1.stem: 30 tiles in 5 rows. Tiles per row?
16 tiles in 4 rows. Tiles per row?
27 tiles, 9 per row. How many rows?
40 tiles in 8 rows. Tiles per row?
An array: 7 rows, ? columns, 49 tiles. How many columns?
Which division does a 3 × 8 array show?

practice_r2.stem: A class has 32 desks in 4 rows. How many desks per row?
Which two equations match a 9 × 6 array?
practice_r2.explanation: 9 × 6 = 54 and 54 ÷ 9 = 6 are related facts for this array.

practice_r2.stem: An array has 48 tiles. One dimension is 8. What is the other?
Which array does NOT have 36 tiles?

practice_r3.stem: Leo makes a rectangular array of 56 stickers. He uses 7 rows. He adds 2 more columns. How many stickers does the new array have?
An array has the same number of rows and columns and has 81 tiles. How many rows?
Kim makes an array with 48 tiles. She can make it with 6 rows or 8 rows. When she uses 6 rows, how many MORE tiles per row compared to 8 rows?
practice_r3.explanation: 6 rows: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 per row. 8 rows: 48 ÷ 8 = 6 per row. 8 − 6 = 2 more.
